titleStmt.title: Letter from Maria Edgeworth to Frances Anne Edgeworth, Unknown
titleStmt.author: Maria Edgeworth
titleStmt.sponsor: Wake Forest University, University of Tennessee, Xavier University, Texas A&M University
titleStmt.principal: Susan Egenolf, Meredith Hale, Hilary Havens, Jessica Richard, and Robin Runia

editionStmt.edition: First digital edition in TEI, date:

availability.p: Reproduced by courtesy of Bodleian Library
availability.licence: Distributed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC BY 4.0) Unported License

msDesc.head:
Letter from
Maria Edgeworth 1768-1849 71477273 62567029
to Frances Anne Edgeworth,

editorialDecl.p: Edgeworth’s spelling and punctuation are retained in transcription, including non-standard spelling, line breaks, and hyphenation of words. The long s and ligatured forms are silently emended.

div.p:
Odd Replies to [underline]odd[/underline]questions [LibNote]60[/LibNote] I asked the Baillies about the princesses - The princess Sophia is not insane - only very nervous & weak - this is the truth and [underline]nervous[/underline] is [underline]not here[/underline] used as a soft equivocal word. The princess Amelia - was said to have died a martyr to the Kings evil & all scars [deletion]and[/deletion] & sores - absolutely false - D[superscript]r. [/superscript]Baillie saw her [insertion]during all her illness[/insertion] [underline]after death[/underline][insertion][unclear][/unclear][/insertion] - told his sister that her neck & every part of her was free from all scar or sore or swelling or any symptom of that disease - She died of atrophy - really worn out - anxiety of mind accelerating or producing it - all about her children true - [insertion]Fitzroy[/insertion] Her husband I [underline]think[/underline] she said but am not [underline]sure - was not[/underline] always kind to her The princess [gap][/gap] is married it is believed to Gen[superscript]l[/superscript]. Garth [deletion]and as[/deletion] Miss Baillie says you w[superscript]d[/superscript] [underline]be surprised[/underline] at her choice if you saw him - All the stories about the Kings apartments being
[underline]stuffed[/underline] to prevent cries being heard [deletion]is [/deletion] false - D[superscript]r.[/superscript] Baillie says the rooms are just as they always were - As to the Queen no mortal knows whether she will die tomorrow - or live months - All the physicians say she may die any hour or may hold out a month or 2 - D[superscript]r.[/superscript] Baillie never sees her - never has seen [underline]her[/underline] since her illness
